commit.message:
hotfix: replace fixed retry delay with exponential backoff and jitter

Stripe outage exposed critical flaw in retry handler. Fixed 3s delay
caused thundering herd when Stripe recovered. New implementation:
- Exponential backoff: base_delay * 2^attempt + random jitter
- Max delay capped at 60s
- Max 5 retry attempts before marking payment as failed

Related: PAY-204, PAY-205

feat: add circuit breaker to retry handler

Adds circuit breaker pattern to prevent cascading failures during
upstream outages. Circuit trips after 5 consecutive failures,
stays open for 30s, then half-opens to test recovery.

Related: PAY-205

test: add comprehensive tests for exponential backoff and circuit breaker

Covers: backoff timing, jitter bounds, max delay cap, circuit breaker
state transitions (closed -> open -> half-open -> closed), and
thundering herd prevention under simulated Stripe outage.

Related: PAY-205

feat: implement TransactionList component with pagination and filtering

Core transaction list view for merchant dashboard redesign.
- Server-side pagination with configurable page size
- Column-based filtering for status, amount, date range
- Sortable columns with visual indicators
- Uses React Query with REST polling (30s interval)

Related: PAY-211

feat: add StatusBadge and DataTable shared components

Reusable UI components for the merchant dashboard:
- StatusBadge: renders payment status with color-coded variants
- DataTable: generic sortable/filterable table with server-side support

Related: PAY-213

feat: add GitHub Actions workflow for production deployments

Replaces Jenkins deploy-production job with GitHub Actions workflow.
Includes: checkout, test, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy,
post-deployment health checks.

Uses reusable workflow pattern for cross-team adoption.
Jenkins pipeline is now deprecated — do not use for new deployments.

Related: PAY-202

chore: mark Jenkinsfile as deprecated

Added deprecation notice to top of Jenkinsfile. All new deployments
should use .github/workflows/deploy-production.yml instead.

Related: PAY-201

fix: handle Stripe webhook clock skew in signature verification

Intermittent signature verification failures caused by ECS task clock
drift. Increased Stripe signature tolerance window from 300s to 600s
as a safety margin. Root cause (NTP sync interval) being fixed by
Marcus on the infrastructure side.

Related: PAY-222

fix: add composite index on payments(merchant_id, created_at)

The get_merchant_transactions query was doing a full table scan on
8M+ rows. Added composite index which brought p99 latency from
4.2s down to 180ms.

Related: PAY-207

feat: add React error boundaries for merchant dashboard sections

Wraps TransactionList, AnalyticsDashboard, and SubscriptionManager
in error boundaries. Each shows a retry button instead of crashing
the entire page.

Related: PAY-223

fix: update NTP sync interval on ECS task definitions

Reduced NTP sync interval from 1 hour to 5 minutes to prevent
clock drift. This was causing Stripe webhook signature verification
failures.

Related: PAY-222

feat: implement token bucket rate limiter with Redis backend

Application-level rate limiting using token bucket algorithm.
- Redis-backed for distributed state
- Per-merchant and per-endpoint configuration
- Billing endpoints get 3x default rate (bursty batch processing)
- Configurable via admin API

Decision made in Thursday architecture review: token bucket over
sliding window due to billing batch processing use case.

Related: PAY-216

docs: add architecture decision record for rate limiting approach

ADR-007: Token Bucket Rate Limiting
- Decision: Use token bucket algorithm over sliding window
- Context: Enterprise partnership launch requires granular rate limiting
- Rationale: Token bucket handles bursty billing batch processing
- Consequences: Slightly more complex merchant-facing documentation

Related: PAY-215

feat: add SQS dead letter queue for failed Stripe webhook events

Failed webhook events are now sent to an SQS DLQ instead of being
silently dropped. A retry Lambda reprocesses events with exponential
backoff. Includes CloudWatch alarm for DLQ depth.

Related: PAY-206

feat: implement WebSocket hook for real-time transaction updates

Replaces REST polling with WebSocket-based real-time updates.
- useTransactionSocket hook manages connection lifecycle
- Automatic reconnection with exponential backoff
- Falls back to REST polling (30s) if WebSocket unavailable
- Integrates with existing React Query cache

Decision from Thursday architecture review: WebSocket preferred
over polling for merchant-facing real-time updates.

Related: PAY-212

feat: add WebSocket endpoint and ALB configuration for merchant dashboard

Adds FastAPI WebSocket endpoint for pushing transaction updates.
Configures ALB listener rule for WebSocket upgrade on /ws/* paths.

Related: PAY-225

refactor: extract payment processor retry logic into shared utility

Moved exponential backoff + circuit breaker into a shared utility
module so it can be reused by the webhook receiver and invoice
generator. Part of the retry pattern audit (PAY-219).

Related: PAY-219

fix: apply exponential backoff to webhook receiver retry logic

Webhook receiver was using fixed 5s retry interval (same bug as
payment processor). Now uses shared resilience utility.

Related: PAY-219

chore: verify and fix Docker Compose setup for fresh clone

Fixed three issues in docker-compose.yml that prevented clean setup:
- Pinned postgres image to 15.4 (latest had breaking changes)
- Added health check for Redis container
- Fixed volume mount path for billing service config

Preparing for new team member onboarding Monday.

Related: PAY-220
